summary: Iteration 10 — Supabase Auth migration bug-fix verification. All five review-request scenarios PASS end-to-end on the preview build. (1) UI LOGIN: qa.captureagent@testmail.dev / CaptureQA#2026 signs in via supabase-js at /login and redirects to /dashboard with sidebar showing 'QA Verification Org / OWNER' and email 'qa.captureagent@testmail.dev' — no 'failed to fetch' or 'invalid credentials'. (2) SESSION + ORG-SCOPED APIs: /opportunities loads exactly 8 rows for QA Verification Org, drawer opens (data-testid opp-drawer), Dashboard renders (MISSION DASHBOARD heading). Full-page reload keeps the user on /opportunities with 8 rows — supabase-js session restore works. (3) BACKEND TOKEN CHAIN (curl): POST {SUPABASE_URL}/auth/v1/token?grant_type=password returned 200 with an access_token for qa.captureagent@testmail.dev; GET https://govcon-workspace.preview.emergentagent.com/api/auth/me with Bearer <token> returned 200 with email 'qa.captureagent@testmail.dev' and organizations=[{id:499e35c6-ca12-4589-aa1a-ae22bdb72c07, name:'QA Verification Org', role:'owner'}] — backend Supabase JWT validation + provision_profile is healthy. (4) FORGOT PASSWORD: /forgot-password → filled forgot-email → clicked forgot-submit → data-testid forgot-done rendered with exact message 'If that account exists, a reset link is on its way to your inbox.' No 'failed to fetch' or 'user not found' errors. (5) NO DATA LOSS (asyncpg read-only): public.users = 15 rows, ALL 15 password_hash values are 60-char bcrypt starting with $2; auth.users = 15 rows; INNER JOIN on public.users.auth_uid = auth.users.id returns 15 rows and 0 mismatches — every user's auth.users.encrypted_password now EQUALS the matching public.users.password_hash, confirming main-agent's hash-repair migration was applied to all 15 users (0 orphan auth rows). (6) REGISTER SMOKE: /register renders its form with 3 input fields and no console errors (no signup performed — respected constraint). ENV FLIP: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL was flipped to https://govcon-workspace.preview.emergentagent.com for the UI tests and FLIPPED BACK to https://captureagent.us with frontend restart before finishing — verified via grep + supervisorctl. No AI-cost endpoints were invoked; no writes to any user's row; only reads on production-shared tables plus one recovery email dispatched to qa.captureagent@testmail.dev (per review-request allowance).
critical_code_review_comments: auth_utils.decode_supabase_token() correctly supports BOTH HS256 (SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET) and asymmetric (JWKS RS256/ES256) — future-proof against Supabase's key rotation. Verified path here is HS256 since Supabase project still signs with the shared secret. Verify_aud is intentionally disabled with clear comment (aud='authenticated').

provision_profile() correctly links pre-existing public.users rows to a new auth_uid via the email fallback branch (line 120-125), which is exactly what enabled the QA account (migrated hash) to sign in without duplicating a users row. Idempotent.

AuthContext.login() calls supabase.auth.signInWithPassword then refreshUser() — the authedRef.current guard (line 14, 30, 35) correctly prevents a late-arriving getSession() bootstrap from clobbering a fresh login. Good defensive coding.

frontend/.env exposes SUPABASE_URL + ANON_KEY as REACT_APP_* — both are safe to expose (anon key is meant for browser use). Confirmed the anon key JWT decodes to role='anon' with the correct project ref.

package.json pin on @supabase/supabase-js should stay at 2.47.10 (Node 18 compatible) — do NOT let it float back to ^2.47.10; 2.110.x requires Node 22 and will break the current production build (main agent already fixed this + regenerated yarn.lock).

Minor: React Router v6 → v7 future-flag warnings still spew in console on every navigation (v7_startTransition, v7_relativeSplatPath). Cosmetic only; suggest adding those flags to the BrowserRouter to silence.

Minor: SUPABASE_URL in backend/.env has a trailing slash ('https://rmhdccypaaemmqdsfhcm.supabase.co/'); auth_utils._get_jwks_client() already .rstrip('/')s it so this is harmless — but worth normalizing at the source.
updated_files: /app/frontend/.env (flipped REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL to preview for tests, restored to https://captureagent.us before finishing — final state verified)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of review-request flows (login, session, opps table, drawer, dashboard, forgot-password, register render)

test_credentials: qa.captureagent@testmail.dev / CaptureQA#2026 — worked via both UI login and direct Supabase GoTrue password grant. Auth uid links to public.users row for QA Verification Org (owner).
seed_data_creation: None. No new users, no new opportunities, no writes to any org. One password-recovery email was dispatched to qa.captureagent@testmail.dev via Supabase (expected side-effect of /forgot-password — explicitly permitted by the review request; delivery + link validity depend on the user's Supabase SMTP+Site-URL settings, out of scope).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Supabase Auth is now the source of truth. QA test creds are unchanged (qa.captureagent@testmail.dev / CaptureQA#2026). DO NOT float @supabase/supabase-js off 2.47.10 (Node 18 constraint). Frontend .env must currently read REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://captureagent.us for deploy — flip to preview before UI testing and flip back before finishing. To exercise auth without hitting Supabase, backend still supports AUTH_TEST_MODE=1 → POST /api/auth/test-login (guarded, off in prod). Regression tests from iter 9 (/app/backend/tests/test_capture_qualification.py) will need an auth token — use the Supabase password-grant + Bearer flow shown in this run to obtain one, or set AUTH_TEST_MODE=1 locally. 15 real users exist in the shared DB — treat non-QA rows as read-only.
rca_of_the_issue: Original user-reported bugs were three cascading issues, all fixed BEFORE this iteration by the main agent: (a) Supabase migration wrote placeholder password hashes into auth.users.encrypted_password, so bcrypt.checkpw() inside GoTrue failed for every real user → 'invalid credentials'. Fix: copied the correct 60-char $2b$ hashes from public.users.password_hash into auth.users.encrypted_password for all 15 users. Verified: 15/15 rows now match on the auth_uid join, 0 mismatches. (b) frontend/.env was missing REACT_APP_SUPABASE_URL + REACT_APP_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, so createClient() in src/lib/supabase.js fell through to createClient('http://localhost','public-anon-key') → 'failed to fetch' on every auth call. Fix: added both vars — supabase-js now targets the real project. (c) package.json used '^@supabase/supabase-js: 2.47.10' with a missing yarn.lock, so a clean install resolved to 2.110.6 which requires Node 22 → production build broke. Fix: pinned exact 2.47.10 + regenerated yarn.lock. All three fixes verified live in this iteration.
